label: implicit_phase3_small_vector_add_128
note: Never-Slower guard: a below-floor (128-element) vector add must stay sequential; the gated parallel dispatcher must not fan out tiny inputs. Compare against implicit_phase1_seq_vector_add_128.

label: implicit_phase3_kernel_seq_add_1000000
note: Sequential lane reference at 1M i64 lanes (memory-bandwidth-bound element-wise add).

label: implicit_phase3_kernel_par_add_1000000
note: Persistent-pool parallel kernel at 1M i64 lanes. Net-faster than the sequential lane on the reference env (4 throttled cores, ~84us thread-wakeup); element-wise i64 is bandwidth-bound so the speedup is sub-linear and only emerges near the 1M materialization cap. PARALLEL_DISPATCH_MIN=900_000 keeps the production floor in this faster region (Never-Slower). Robust scaling target is a compute-bound kernel (matrix multiply) in a follow-up.
